prompt: Investigate the mass-directory-move link-repair regression in Demon Docs. Do not edit files. Focus only on why an initialized repo with document_id frontmatter can move six documentation trees and then `demon fix` reports 139 ambiguous links instead of following prior TargetFileID identities. Inspect current source and recent commits around internal/links reconciliation/state/inventory. Consider whether moved source files lose previous link records, whether document identity aliases collapse correctly, and whether recent state-refresh or Obsidian-path changes caused this. Return a concise root-cause hypothesis with exact functions/conditions and the smallest safe code change plus regression-test shape.

result.final_message:
Most likely root cause: prior link records are matched by destination text, not just source/link identity.

- `reconcileMarkdownSource` calls `findPreviousLink` at [reconcile.go:415](https://github.com/Lokee86/demon-docs/blob/main/internal/links/reconcile.go#L415).
- `findPreviousLink` requires exact `Target` and `Syntax` matches at [reconcile.go:560](https://github.com/Lokee86/demon-docs/blob/main/internal/links/reconcile.go#L560).
- If Obsidian rewrites relative/wiki paths during the directory move, the match fails. `previous.TargetFileID` is then empty, so the preferred-ID branch at line 361 is skipped and `candidatePathsForSyntax` falls back to basename/path candidates, producing the 139 ambiguities.

A moved source itself should not lose records when its `FileRecord.ID` survives: `sourceUnchanged` intentionally fails on a path move, but `previousBySource[source.record.ID]` still supplies the old records. The document-ID machinery is otherwise sound: [inventory.go](https://github.com/Lokee86/demon-docs/blob/main/internal/links/inventory.go#L45) matches unique IDs, and [document_aliases.go](https://github.com/Lokee86/demon-docs/blob/main/internal/links/document_aliases.go#L12) remaps one-present/one-absent duplicates. The existing `f79ee9a` fix and mass-move test cover that path.

There is also a real secondary defect in incremental refresh: [TrackSources](https://github.com/Lokee86/demon-docs/blob/main/internal/links/scoped_tracking.go#L20) and `refreshScopedFile` at line 130 are path-only. A moved source gets a new random ID, leaving old source records keyed to the old ID. This can corrupt state before a later fix, but link-enabled `demon fix` normally uses full `Reconcile`, so it is probably an amplifier rather than the direct 139-link cause.

Smallest safe change: add a narrowly guarded fallback after exact matching for a unique `(ordinal, syntax)` record when its prior `TargetFileID` resolves to the old target path/history or otherwise proves a move. Do not use unconditional ordinal matching, since that could preserve identity across an intentional target replacement.

Regression shape:

1. Baseline source/target files with unique `document_id` values and saved `TargetFileID`.
2. Move six trees and alter link spellings as Obsidian would.
3. Run `Reconcile`/`demon fix`; assert zero ambiguity, unchanged `TargetFileID`, and the expected rewritten paths.
4. Separately test `TrackSources` on a moved source and assert its original file ID and source link records remain, with no duplicate present old/new records.

No files were edited; local PowerShell execution was unavailable, so tests were not run.
